title: Operating Method

hierarchy.label: 1. Physics & geography
hierarchy.body: Straits do not move. Thermodynamics does not negotiate. Highest predictability.

hierarchy.label: 2. Demography
hierarchy.body: 2044's workforce is born. Fertility is the only free variable left. 20-yr forecasts work here and almost nowhere else.

hierarchy.label: 3. Installed base & lead times
hierarchy.body: A fab takes 4-5 yrs, a mine 17, a transmission line 12. The near future's supply side is already determined by today's order books.

hierarchy.label: 4. Institutions & treaties
hierarchy.body: Montreux, FERC, BIS rules. Slow-changing but human — can break at stress points.

hierarchy.label: 5. Prices & capital flows
hierarchy.body: Reflexive: observation changes them. Months of validity at best. Trade them; don't anchor worldviews to them.

hierarchy.label: 6. Narratives & sentiment
hierarchy.body: Days-to-weeks. The froth layer. Useful only as a contrarian sensor and timing overlay.

rules: Find the binding constraint; ignore the rest until it moves. Theory of constraints applies to civilization.
Lead time = the future's skeleton. Anything physical arriving in 2029 was ordered by 2026. Read order books, not forecasts.
Watch quantities before prices: AIS transits, fab utilization, queue lengths, backlog/book-to-bill. Prices are the lagging, reflexive shadow.
Insurance is the most honest forecaster on Earth — real money, daily marks, no narrative budget.
Every chokepoint creates its own bypass given time + incentive (Arm→RISC-V, SWIFT→CIPS, cobalt→LFP). Rents decay; date-stamp them.
Announcement ≠ steel. Count megawatts energized and wafers out, not dollars in press releases. The ratio is the hype-meter.
Constraint breeds invention (DeepSeek under export controls). Model the squeezed party's R&D response, not just their pain.
Subsidized inefficiency is strategy, not stupidity (SMIC yields, Indonesia's ore ban). States pay tuition for capability.
Second-order beats first-order: Houthis → Cape route → tanker tonne-miles → shipowner profits. The third hop is where mispricing lives.
Reflexivity caps all of this: a map good enough to trade on gets arbitraged into uselessness. Edge = update speed, not the static map.
biases: Anchoring: did I generate my own number before reading consensus?
Confirmation: what would CHANGE my mind? Is it written down with a date?
Sunk cost: would I enter this position today at this price?
Narrative: am I holding a story or a mechanism? Can I draw the causal arrows?
Recency: is this a trend or a cycle? Where are we in the cycle?
Survivorship: am I studying winners only? Where are the dead bodies (Lucent, Nortel, WorldCom)?

sources.label: LBNL Queued Up + DC load report
UN World Population Prospects 2024
sources.note: the only reliable 20-yr forecasts in existence

sources.note: the bond market of the AI-power thesis
